data.title: PennantPark Announces October 2025 Distribution
data.description:
Pennantpark $ ( (PFLT) ) has issued an announcement.

On October 2, 2025, PennantPark Floating Rate Capital Ltd. announced a monthly distribution of $0.1025 per share for October 2025, payable on November 3, 2025, to stockholders of record as of October 15, 2025. This distribution is expected to be paid from taxable net investment income, with specific tax characteristics to be reported to stockholders on Form 1099 after the calendar year ends. The announcement highlights the company’s ongoing commitment to providing returns to its investors and its strategic positioning within the investment landscape, particularly in offering tax-efficient distributions to non-U.S. stockholders.

data.title: Reed’s Plans Reverse Stock Split Amid SEC Filing
data.description:
An announcement from Reed’s ( (REED) ) is now available.

On September 29, 2025, D&D Source of Life Holding Ltd., a major stockholder of Reed’s, Inc., authorized the Board of Directors to amend the company’s certificate of incorporation to implement a reverse stock split at a ratio between 1-for-3 and 1-for-9. This decision, which does not affect the total authorized shares, was followed by the filing of a preliminary Information Statement with the SEC on September 30, 2025. The reverse stock split will take effect after the filing of a definitive Information Statement and subsequent amendment to the charter, impacting the company’s stock structure and potentially its market positioning.

The most recent analyst rating on (REED) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Reed’s stock, see the REED Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Veea Faces Nasdaq Noncompliance Notices in September 2025
data.description:
Veea ( (VEEA) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On September 29, 2025, Veea Inc. received notices from Nasdaq regarding noncompliance with three listing requirements: the minimum bid price, the market value of publicly held shares, and the market value of listed securities. These notices do not immediately affect the company’s listing status, but Veea has been given 180 days, until March 30, 2026, to regain compliance. Failure to meet these requirements could lead to delisting, although Veea has options to appeal or transfer its listing to the Nasdaq Capital Market, which has reduced compliance standards.

More about Veea
